blockingIssues.description: Built-in payload partial-move rejection is still incomplete for nested Option/Result payloads, so some owned enum payload bindings still bypass the whole-binding ownership rule.
Final-expression Ok/Err/Some/None fast paths still return directly without return-cleanup or consumed-guard suppression, so nested cleanup and exactly-once ownership behavior remain incomplete.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Clarify that `skillFeedback.followedProcedure` must be false whenever workers skip documented tests-first or ownership-validation requirements, especially the negative-compile-plus-positive-runtime expectation for ownership fixes.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Both round-3 fix reviews found `followedProcedure: true` despite transcript/handoff evidence of documented procedure deviations: `ownership-trait-dispatch-and-builtin-pattern-boundaries` edited implementation before tests, and `ownership-return-fast-path-and-loop-exit-cleanup` recorded only positive runtime coverage despite the ownership validation requirement calling for a negative compile path plus a positive runtime path.
